    SampleSales — Audit & Health Report

    BI architects, technical leads, and governance teams · generated TIMESTAMP

    Documentation Completeness: 6% 16 fields awaiting input

    Missing: Owner, Refresh Schedule, Version, Status, Author, +11 more

    86/100
    Health Score
    Good
    Band
    Low
    Complexity
    14
    Findings

    This model scores 86/100 overall (Good), with low structural complexity across 4 tables, 4 measures, and 3 relationships. Modeling is the area holding the score back (75/100), while performance is the strongest area (97/100). 4 DAX finding(s), 5 failed best-practice check(s), 3 performance risk signal(s), and 7 governance finding(s) were identified. The top priority is: Some relationships use bidirectional cross-filtering. (High) — Switch to single-direction filtering and use CROSSFILTER() in specific DAX measures where bidirectional behavior is truly needed. ```csharp // Tabular Editor C# Script to resolve bidirectional cross-filtering Model.Relationships.First(rel => rel.FromTable.Name == "Sales" && rel.ToTable.Name == "Date").CrossFilteringBehavior = CrossFilteringBehavior.OneDirection; ```

    86/100

    Performance973 performance risk signal(s) (worst severity: Low).
    Unused Assets7612 unused asset(s) add size and maintenance load without analytical value.

    4. Model Best Practices

    CheckResultDetail
    Star schemaPassModel follows a star schema — a star schema centred on the 'Sales' fact table.
    Fact/dimension separationPass1 fact table(s), 2 dimension table(s).
    Table naming conventionsPassNo generic/default table names detected.
    Display folder usagePass4/4 measures use a display folder.
    Description coverageFail0/17 measures and visible columns have a description.
    Dedicated date tablePass'Date' looks like the model's date table.
    Hidden technical/key columnsFail3/6 ID/key-like columns are hidden.
    Unused calculated columnsFail1 calculated column(s) are not referenced by any visual: Sales[LineTotal].
    Inactive relationshipsFail1 inactive relationship(s) — only reachable via USERELATIONSHIP().
    Bidirectional cross-filteringFail1 relationship(s) use bidirectional cross-filtering, which can create ambiguous filter paths.
    Many-to-many relationshipsPassNo many-to-many relationships.
    No circular relationship pathsPassNo circular relationship paths detected.
    No disconnected fact/dimension tablesPassEvery fact/dimension table participates in at least one relationship.
    No development leftovers in production modelPassNo table/column names match common development-leftover patterns.
    Summarize-by setting on key columnsPassKey/ID columns have SummarizeBy set to None.
    Snowflake dimension depth limitPassAll dimension tables are within recommended chain depth of 2.
    Column naming conventions (typos)PassNo typos detected in column names.

    5. Performance Risks

    All performance risks below are heuristics inferred from metadata only — no row-level data is ever extracted, so nothing here reflects a measured runtime.

    KindObjectTableSeverityDetail
    High Cardinality SignalCustomerKeySalesLow'CustomerKey' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    High Cardinality SignalOrderDateKeySalesLow'OrderDateKey' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    High Cardinality SignalShipDateKeySalesLow'ShipDateKey' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.

    6. Governance

    AreaSeverityDetail
    RLSLowRole 'Regional Manager' has no members assigned in the model file (membership may be managed in the Power BI Service).
    DescriptionsMediumOnly 0% of measures/visible columns have a description.
    OwnershipMediumNo owner or data classification was specified when this documentation was generated.
    Unused PagesLowReport page 'Data Quality' is hidden and has no drill-through targets configured (potentially unused leftover).
    Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Sales' and 'Customer' references a hidden column.
    Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Sales' and 'Date' references a hidden column.
    Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Sales' and 'Date' references a hidden column.

    7. Unused Assets

    Asset TypeCountItems
    Measures2Revenue YTD, Orphan Margin
    Columns8Customer[Customer Name], Date[Month Name], Sales[CustomerKey], Sales[OrderDateKey], Sales[ShipDateKey], Sales[Quantity], Sales[UnitPrice], Sales[Status]
    Tables0—
    Calculated columns1Sales[LineTotal]
    Report pages1Data Quality

    Hierarchies and calculation groups are not yet parsed by PBICompass, so they are excluded from this audit.

    8. Recommendations

    High Some relationships use bidirectional cross-filtering.

    Why it matters: Bidirectional filters can create ambiguous filter paths and materially slow down query performance.

    Suggested fix: Switch to single-direction filtering and use CROSSFILTER() in specific DAX measures where bidirectional behavior is truly needed.

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to resolve bidirectional cross-filtering
Model.Relationships.First(rel => rel.FromTable.Name == "Sales" && rel.ToTable.Name == "Date").CrossFilteringBehavior = CrossFilteringBehavior.OneDirection;

    Expected benefit: More predictable filtering and better query performance.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    Medium Many measures/columns have no description.

    Why it matters: Missing descriptions mean new team members must reverse-engineer intent from DAX and column names alone.

    Suggested fix: Add descriptions to the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and self-documenting metadata in Power BI Desktop tooltips.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium Row-level security gaps were found.

    Why it matters: Without RLS (or with roles that have no members configured), sensitive rows may be visible to more users than intended.

    Suggested fix: Define RLS roles for any data that should be restricted, and assign members either in the model or in the Power BI Service.

    Expected benefit: Data access that matches the intended audience.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    Medium Description coverage across measures and columns is low.

    Why it matters: Missing descriptions increase onboarding time and the risk of misinterpreting a field's meaning.

    Suggested fix: Prioritize descriptions for the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misunderstandings.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium No owner or data classification was specified for this report.

    Why it matters: Without a named owner, there's no clear point of contact for questions, incidents, or change requests.

    Suggested fix: Assign a business owner and a data classification (e.g. Internal, Confidential) in the documentation metadata.

    Expected benefit: Clear accountability and appropriate handling of the data.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some measures have no description.

    Why it matters: Without a description, business meaning has to be reverse-engineered from the DAX every time.

    Suggested fix: Add a one-sentence description to each measure explaining what it calculates and why.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misinterpretations of what a measure means.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some ID/key-style columns are visible to report authors.

    Why it matters: Visible technical join keys clutter the field list and can be mis-used in visuals by mistake.

    Suggested fix: Hide columns that exist only to support relationships.

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to hide key/ID columns
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["CustomerKey"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["OrderDateKey"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["ShipDateKey"].IsHidden = true;

    Expected benefit: A cleaner field list focused on business-relevant fields.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some calculated columns are never used in any report visual.

    Why it matters: Unused calculated columns still consume model size and refresh time for no analytical benefit.

    Suggested fix: Remove calculated columns that are not referenced anywhere, or confirm they are needed for row-level security/relationships.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and faster refreshes.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some relationships are inactive.

    Why it matters: Inactive relationships only take effect via USERELATIONSHIP() in DAX — easy to forget and easy to misconfigure.

    Suggested fix: Document where each inactive relationship is activated, or make it the active relationship if that's the common case.

    Expected benefit: Less risk of a report silently using the wrong relationship.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some visible columns look like high-cardinality identifiers.

    Why it matters: High-cardinality columns compress poorly and can slow down the model if used in visuals or relationships.

    Suggested fix: Hide the column if it's a technical key, or confirm its cardinality is acceptable for the intended use.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and better query performance, if confirmed unnecessary.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low 12 unused asset(s) were found (measures, columns, tables, calculated columns, or hidden non-drillthrough pages).

    Why it matters: Unused assets add to model size, refresh time, and cognitive load for anyone maintaining the report.

    Suggested fix: Remove unused assets, or confirm they're intentionally kept for a documented reason (e.g. future use, RLS support).

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to delete unused assets
Model.Tables["Customer"].Columns["Customer Name"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Date"].Columns["Month Name"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["CustomerKey"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["OrderDateKey"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["ShipDateKey"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["Quantity"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["UnitPrice"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["Status"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["LineTotal"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Measures["Revenue YTD"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Measures["Orphan Margin"].Delete();

    Expected benefit: A leaner, easier-to-maintain model.

    Estimated effort: Low
